---

## Executive Summary

The May 21, 2026 Reliability Committee meeting is dominated by two market-significant developments. First, ISO-NE advanced its Capacity Auction Reforms – Seasonal/Accreditation (CAR-SA, Item 5) framework, presenting the proposed triennial Seasonal Tie Benefits (STB) study cycle. Stakeholder questions are due **May 29, 2026**, with a Q4 2026 FERC filing targeted.

Second, the RC will vote on a package of operational procedure changes (Item 7), including the new Storage as Transmission-Only Assets (SATOA) framework.

---

## Agenda Item Summaries

### Item 2: Reliability Committee Consent Agenda

The consent agenda is notification-only and contains seven PPA actions: two new Level 0 solar PV applications, one Level I transmission PPA, and four withdrawals removing approximately 17.624 MW of solar PV from the ISO-NE queue.

---

### Item 3: Level II/III Proposed Plan Applications

Three Level III PPAs are before the committee for 66.67% votes. All are CEII-protected; substantive reliability implications are not disclosed in the public materials.

---

### Item 5: Capacity Auction Reforms – Seasonal/Accreditation (CAR-SA)

This is the highest-relevance item on the agenda. ISO-NE presented two detailed CAR-SA components — the proposed triennial STB study cycle and adjustments to the Capacity Zone formation process.

**Figure:** STB process timeline showing the Q3 2027 comprehensive study anchoring the three-year cycle.
